heading: Canadian vessels aiding vessels in United States waters
label: 80105 Canadian vessels aiding vessels in United States waters
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)8010546 App.:725., ; , ; (last par. under heading “Department of State”), .June 19, 1878, ch. 32420 Stat. 175May 24, 1890, ch. 29226 Stat. 120Mar. 3, 1893, ch. 211, § 127 Stat. 683In subsection (a), before paragraph (1), the word “equipment” is substituted for “appurtenance” to use more commonly understood language. The words “and assistance” are omitted as unnecessary.Subsection (b) is substituted for “this section shall cease to be in force and effect from and after the date of the proclamation of the President of the United States to the effect that said reciprocol privilege has been withdrawn, revoked, or rendered inoperative by the said Government of the Dominion of Canada” to eliminate unnecessary words.

The words “and assistance” are omitted as unnecessary.Subsection (b) is substituted for “this section shall cease to be in force and effect from and after the date of the proclamation of the President of the United States to the effect that said reciprocol privilege has been withdrawn, revoked, or rendered inoperative by the said Government of the Dominion of Canada” to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.text: the canal and improvement of the waters between Lake Erie and Lake Huron; and
the Saint Marys River and canal.

children.heading: .—Reciprocity
children.label: b .—Reciprocity
children.text: This section does not apply after the President proclaims that privileges reciprocal to those under subsection (a) have been withdrawn or rendered inoperative by the Government of Canada.
